body_text: FOLLOW-UP NEEDED, customer is waiting on a team answer.
Deye 6KW Off-Grid Inverter. Customer wants details on it, no price or stock confirmed yet, needs a team reply.

_notes.purpose: Permanent-delivery version of the owner / sales-desk escalation alert. Lets Sunny reach an owner OR a sales desk even when that number has been silent for more than 24h (free-form sends are silently dropped outside the 24h customer-service window; approved templates are not). ONE template covers every recipient and every escalation type because src/owner_alert.js > buildOwnerAlertText produces an identical body for all of them, with only the header line varying. The matching template parameters are built by buildOwnerAlertTemplateComponents in the same file.
_notes.covers: Both the owner alerts (Patrick / Charbel, big projects) AND the sales-desk alerts (Abuja Sales / Lagos Sales) AND the repeat follow-up ping. The recipient is chosen by the router (src/owner_routing.js); the message body is the same template, only {{1}} (header) and the routed phone differ.
_notes.category_reason: UTILITY: an operational notification tied to a live lead, not marketing.

variables.{{1}} (BODY): typed header line from ESCALATION_HEADERS (HOT LEAD / FOLLOW-UP NEEDED / NEGOTIATION / BIG PROJECT / BULK ORDER / LIVE AGENT / etc.), or the repeat-ping header.
variables.{{3}} (BODY): the situation summary, flattened to a single line by the component builder (Meta rejects newlines inside a variable). The product (when known) is prepended to the summary here instead of being its own variable, to keep the variable count down for Meta's parameters-to-words ratio. The fallback one-liner is used for synthetic classifications.
variables.{{4}} (BODY): the full wa.me follow-up link, e.g. 'https://wa.me/2348034455038?text=Hello...'. Tappable in the delivered message; the owner taps it, reviews the pre-filled draft, and sends.

_notes.code_wiring: src/handler.js > sendOwnerAlert tries this template FIRST (window-independent) and falls back to the free-form sendMessage if the template send fails (covers the PENDING-approval period, a missing OWNER_ALERT_TEMPLATE env, or any Meta rejection), so the deploy is safe regardless of approval state. Used by notifyOwnerEscalation (main alert) and the repeat-ping branch. The readable multi-line free-form text is still what gets persisted to the Owner Chat thread. Template name/lang overridable via OWNER_ALERT_TEMPLATE / OWNER_ALERT_TEMPLATE_LANG.
design_decisions: Send template ALWAYS (template-first, free-form fallback), matching the proven nightly_audit_ping_en pattern. The whole point is to escape the 24h window, so we do not try to detect whether the recipient's window is open.
One shared template, not one-per-recipient: the bodies are byte-identical except the variable header, so a second template would add a Meta approval for zero message difference (decided 2026-06-24).
BODY is prefixed with a fixed sentence so it does not start with a variable (a known Meta rejection trigger).

NO URL button: Meta rejects wa.me destinations in URL buttons (error_subcode 2388081, 'Direct links to WhatsApp aren't allowed for buttons', confirmed on submit 2026-06-24). The follow-up link is therefore carried as a body variable, which renders as a tappable link in the delivered message (same as the free-form alert, which puts the link in body text). This is why the template has no BUTTONS component.

FOUR variables, not five: a 5-variable version was rejected with error_subcode 2388293 'Parameters words ratio exceeds limit' (too many variables for the fixed-text length, confirmed on submit 2026-06-24). The dedicated Product variable was dropped and the product is now prepended to the Summary ({{3}}) by buildOwnerAlertTemplateComponents, and the fixed scaffolding text was lengthened, to satisfy Meta's parameters-to-words ratio.

BODY must not end on a variable: a version ending '...tap this link: {{4}}' was rejected with error_subcode 2388299 'Leading or trailing params not allowed' (confirmed on submit 2026-06-24). A fixed trailing sentence ('You can also open the Electro-Sun admin inbox...') was added after {{4}} so neither the first nor the last element is a variable.
_notes.how_to_submit: META_WABA_ID must be the live WABA (986225450549617) in .env. Already added to TEMPLATE_FILES in scripts/submit_templates.js. Run: node scripts/submit_templates.js  then check status with: node scripts/check_templates.js
